title: reactullm-sdd runtime LLM contract
description: Language-neutral runtime contract between a shipped reactullm-sdd React frontend and pyllum (Python async LLM framework). This is the RUNTIME twin of reactullm-pyllum.contract.json (which governs build-time test planning): it governs LIVE calls made by the app's end users. The frontend builds an LLMRequest and POSTs it; pyllum validates it against `request_schema`, routes on `task`, sets the system prompt to `instructions` (or the task default), runs ONE pyllum turn constrained to `schema` when present, and replies with a `response_schema`-valid LLMResponse. Wire keys are camelCase. Neither repo imports the other. GENERATED from src/runtime.ts by `npm run gen:runtime` on the reactullm side — do not hand-edit; regenerate and bump `version`.

seam.role: reactullm's runtime client: build LLMRequest, POST, consume LLMResponse

operations.with_instructions: set the system prompt to `instructions`, or the task's server-side default when empty (fluent)
operations.with_schema: when `schema` is non-null, constrain the response to it (fluent)
operations.ask: run ONE turn on `input`; return `data` (structured) or `text` (free-form)

seam.invariant: On ok=true exactly one of data/text is non-null, chosen by whether the request carried a schema. On ok=false both are null and error is set.

task.description: Stable id the server routes/authorizes/prompts on, e.g. 'summarize_job'. Not free-form prose; both sides agree on it.

input.description: The user turn / content to act on. Passed to the turn's ask(...).

instructions.description: System prompt. Empty -> the server supplies the default system prompt registered for this task.

schema.description: A JSON Schema object, or null. Non-null -> structured-output turn; response `data` must honor it. Null -> free-form; response `text` carries the answer.

variables.description: Named values interpolated into the task's prompt template. Travels from the browser -> untrusted, MUST NOT carry secrets; the server allow-lists which keys it honors per task.

task.description: Echo of the request `task`, so out-of-order replies are matchable.

ok.description: true -> success payload (data or text) present; false -> error present.

data.description: Structured result honoring the request `schema`. Non-null only when the request had a schema AND ok=true.

text.description: Free-form answer. Non-null only when the request had NO schema AND ok=true.

message.description: Human-readable detail. Never leaks stack traces, keys, or secrets.

notes: `task` is the authorization key: the browser MUST NOT choose the model or inject arbitrary system prompts the server did not register. Unknown task -> error.code=unknown_task.
`variables` is untrusted browser input; the server honors only an allow-list of keys per task and never echoes secrets.
Wire keys stay camelCase (the request 'schema' key is reserved in some frameworks; alias it rather than renaming the shape).
